release_review.summary: Redeven v0.8.0 combines published Flowersec v0.20.2 and Floe Webapp v0.37.1 transport hardening, published Floret v0.4.0 model-directed concurrency, and Floeterm coverage snapshots. Terminal history requires a matched v0.8.0 Desktop and Runtime pair so local sessions become interactive after the committed baseline without weakening Flower, security, or process-lifecycle boundaries.
checked_surfaces: Remote Flowersec Direct and Tunnel connections explicitly require TLS, Local UI Direct connections explicitly allow plaintext only for canonical loopback authorities, and the Docker integration client uses the same loopback-only exception

Flowersec Go and TypeScript v0.20.2 and Floe Webapp v0.37.1 are consumed only from published artifacts with GOWORK=off validation and no replace, go.work, package-manager link, workspace alias, or local sibling path wiring

Acknowledged Yamux liveness, 64 KiB encrypted-record chunks, WebSocket watermarks, Yamux stream and receive-memory limits, and bounded RPC workers and queues change transport resource behavior without changing Redeven RPC type IDs or payload schemas

Flowersec v0.20.2 consumes authenticated handshake state once, bounds outbound and proxy queues, propagates cancellation and session termination, and retains ConnectArtifact v1, E2EE record framing, Yamux wire behavior, RPC type IDs, and proxy.runtime@1

Redeven blocks only Content-Security-Policy, Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only, and X-Frame-Options on proxied product surfaces; X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, COOP, COEP, and CORP continue through Flowersec's default response policy

Env App reconnects use refreshable ArtifactSource acquire functions and stable reconnect config objects so credentials remain one-use and trace context survives hard reconnects without changing session metadata or Runtime Service envelopes

Flower consumes only published Floret v0.4.0 for model-directed concurrent tool batches while transcript ordering, cancellation, permission, serialized user approval, audit, control-signal, projection recovery, token-budget, deletion-fencing, and activity-summary boundaries remain internal to Flower and Floret

Runtime Service protocol_version remains redeven-runtime-v1, while compatibility_epoch advances to 6 because terminal.history now requires explicit retained-floor, committed-coverage, snapshot-end, history-generation, reset, and truncation metadata
The Runtime consumes only the published github.com/floegence/floeterm/terminal-go v0.4.23 module with GOWORK=off validation and no replace, go.work, or local sibling path wiring

Env App consumes only the published @floegence/floeterm-terminal-web 0.5.18 package and maps the Redeven terminal.history response into the released coordinator contract without a product-local recovery state machine
Initial terminal input becomes available only after the history baseline has crossed the terminal parser completion fence; retry and degraded recovery after that baseline remain non-blocking

Missing, malformed, stale-generation, reset, and retention-truncated history contracts fail or rebase through structured coordinator states instead of inferring coverage from retained chunks or rendering raw internal errors
Desktop and Runtime minimum versions advance together to v0.8.0 so a new Env App cannot silently attach to an older Runtime that omits required coverage metadata
Local transport and credential boundaries, execute permission enforcement, managed Runtime process reconciliation, Activity surface lifecycle, and the Floret review boundary remain intact

Desktop attach/start, structured startup reports, sys.ping, Local UI health, Env App maintenance routes, gateway lifecycle, SSH Runtime bootstrap, installer discovery, and provider request shaping remain otherwise unchanged
